source: HMC_Package_Catalogue.pdf — 65 packages verified against the PDF tables on pages 3-8. Scenario C prices = round(B × 1.8 / 10) × 10 per the spec.

categories.name: Gastroenteritis, GI & IV Therapy
categories.description: Acute gastroenteritis is the most common presentation in the Red Sea region (~60% of tourist hospital cases). This combined category covers GI complaints together with IV hydration therapy, as they are clinically linked. Packages are tiered by severity, hydration status, and whether laboratory work or extended observation is required.
categories.escalationNote: Persistent vomiting beyond reasonable limits, severe dehydration unresponsive to IV, bloody diarrhea, signs of acute abdomen, or pediatric cases under 1 year of age are escalated to inpatient admission. Assistance company notified within 15 minutes.

categories.name: Respiratory Conditions
categories.description: Acute respiratory infections — bronchitis, pharyngitis, tonsillitis, pneumonia — are among the most frequent presentations in travelers visiting the Red Sea region. Packages cover mild outpatient cases through acute exacerbations requiring nebulizer therapy and supportive treatment.
categories.escalationNote: Severe respiratory distress, suspected acute COVID-19 with hypoxia, or paediatric stridor are escalated to §11 admission pathway. Assistance company notified within 15 minutes.

categories.name: Wound Care & Suturing
categories.description: Wound care packages are tiered by wound complexity, suture count, anatomical location, and whether imaging is required to exclude underlying injury. Sea-urchin removal is included given its frequency in Red Sea tourist medicine.
categories.escalationNote: Wounds with deep tissue involvement, suspected nerve or tendon damage, animal bites, or wounds older than 12 hours may require additional procedures and are quoted separately.

categories.name: Trauma & Orthopedic
categories.description: Trauma cases — sprains, contusions, suspected fractures — are common in active tourist environments. HMC's portable X-Ray capability allows full assessment at the hotel for travelers unable to travel, with cast or immobilization performed on site.
categories.escalationNote: Open fractures, suspected spinal injury, multi-trauma, or compartment-syndrome signs are escalated to §11 ambulance pathway with receiving-hospital coordination.

categories.name: ENT (Ear · Nose · Throat)
categories.description: ENT presentations include ear barotrauma (common after diving), otitis from swimming, and acute throat infections. Ear wash and foreign-body extraction are routinely performed using portable equipment.
categories.escalationNote: Severe epistaxis unresponsive to anterior packing, suspected mastoiditis, deep neck infection, or hearing loss with neurological signs are escalated to §11 specialist pathway.

categories.name: Dental Emergency
categories.description: Dental packages cover urgent presentations only. Insurance coverage for dental services varies by plan — pre-authorization is recommended for any procedure beyond consultation.
categories.escalationNote: Maxillofacial trauma, deep facial-space infection, airway-threatening swelling, or oral bleeding refractory to local measures are escalated to §11 oral-surgery pathway.

categories.name: Allergic & Skin Conditions
categories.description: Sun exposure, marine environment, and dietary changes lead to a high incidence of allergic and dermatological presentations during travel.
categories.escalationNote: Anaphylaxis, full-thickness or extensive burns, suspected Stevens-Johnson syndrome, or severe systemic reactions are escalated to §11 ambulance pathway.

categories.name: Eye Conditions
categories.description: Conjunctivitis from sun, chlorine, or sand is the dominant eye presentation. Foreign-body removal is performed in clinic under magnification.
categories.escalationNote: Penetrating eye injury, sudden vision loss, suspected acute angle-closure glaucoma, or chemical burn to the eye are escalated to §11 ophthalmology specialist pathway with receiving-hospital coordination.

categories.name: Cardiac Outpatient
categories.description: Cardiac outpatient packages are designed for low-risk presentations only. ALL chest pain or suspected cardiac events undergo immediate ECG screening, and any abnormality triggers immediate escalation to ER and notification to the assistance company.
categories.escalationNote: Mandatory escalation: ST changes on ECG, troponin elevation, hemodynamic instability, SpO₂ < 94%, or unresolved chest pain → immediate transfer to ER with ambulance support. Assistance company notified within 15 minutes.
